data.title: SSL/TLS Encryption
data.description: Google.com uses SSL/TLS to encrypt data transmitted between users and servers.
data.technical_spec: Employs strong encryption standards such as AES-256.

data.title: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
data.description: Provides an additional layer of security for user accounts.
data.technical_spec: Supports multiple 2FA methods including mobile push notifications.

data.title: Advanced Threat Protection
data.description: Leverages machine learning to detect and mitigate potential threats in real-time.
data.technical_spec: Monitors system behavior to identify patterns indicative of an attack.

data.title: Security Patch Management
data.description: Regular updates and patches are applied to fix vulnerabilities.
data.technical_spec: Automated deployment of patches across global infrastructure.

data.title: DDoS Mitigation
data.description: Implements measures to protect against distributed denial-of-service attacks.
data.technical_spec: Uses load balancing and anti-DDoS technologies for attack absorption.
